name: Almond Butter
description: Pure ground almonds only — no added sugar, oils, or preservatives.

name: Almond Flour (Base)
description: Fine house-milled almond flour from whole raw almonds
instructions: Step 1: Blitz whole raw almonds in a high-speed blender until a fine, fluffy flour forms.\nStep 2: Sift once to remove any larger pieces; use immediately or store chilled.

name: Almond Milk (Unsweetened)
description: Clean unsweetened almond milk
instructions: Dairy-free liquid base.

name: Apple Cider Beet Marinade (Base)
description: Tangy apple cider vinaigrette for beet and fruit salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk vinegar and olive oil together.\nStep 2: Toss with prepared salad and rest 15–20 minutes.

name: Avocado-Mango Salsa (Base)
description: Fresh avocado-mango salsa with pepper, onion, cilantro, and lime — optional chilli for heat.
instructions: Step 1: Dice mango, avocado, pepper, and onion; rinse onion and drain.\nStep 2: Chop coriander and mince chilli if using.\nStep 3: Fold together with lime juice, coconut water, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Rest 10 minutes before serving.

name: Base Soup
description: Line1 Line2, comma
instructions: Step 1\nStep 2

name: Basil Pesto (House)
description: Clean nut-free herbaceous basil pesto fluid
instructions: Step 1: Place fresh basil leaves, raw garlic, and sea salt into a food processor.\nStep 2: Pulse while slowly streaming in extra virgin olive oil until a vibrant, smooth green emulsion forms.

name: Beef Bones (Feet)
description: Beef marrow bones and feet for long-simmered broth; solids are strained out after cooking.

name: Beef Ground Lean
description: Lean ground beef, raw
instructions: Lean ground beef sourced for clean whole-food profiles.

name: Beef Rib Bones (Trimmed)
description: Bone-in rib bones with all visible meat removed before roasting for broth.

name: Beef Ribs
description: Bone-in beef ribs for roasting and broth extraction.

name: Black Tamarind (Dried)
description: Sour dried black tamarind pulp for tandoori and Thai marinades.

name: Blackened Seasoning (Base)
description: Classic Cajun blackened spice rub with smoked paprika, garlic, onion, oregano, thyme, and cayenne heat.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk together 2 tsp smoked paprika, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp sea salt, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp dried thyme, ½ tsp black pepper, and ¼–½ tsp cayenne pepper until evenly blended.\nStep 2: Store airtight. Use as a dry rub on fish, chicken, or vegetables before high-heat searing.

name: Bone Broth (Base)
description: Long-simmered, defatted beef bone broth — nutrition is per 100 ml; one serving is 500 ml.
instructions:
Step 1: Trim and scrape all visible meat from the rib bones. Roast beef bones (feet) and trimmed rib bones at 200°C until deeply golden.
Step 2: Transfer to a large pot and cover with cold filtered water.
Step 3: Add 1.5g sea salt (about ¼ tsp), black pepper, and apple cider vinegar — keep the batch lightly seasoned.
Step 4: Add carrots, celery, white onion, cinnamon, rosemary, thyme, star anise, and whole black lime (loomi).
Step 5: Simmer on the lowest heat for at least 18 hours.
Step 6: Strain through a fine mesh sieve; discard all solids.
Step 7: Cool completely, refrigerate overnight, and skim off all fat from the surface.
Step 8: Reheat only the defatted broth to serve. Season individual cups with an extra pinch of salt only if needed. Batch yield is about 3000 ml.

name: Brown Herbal Rice (Base)
description: Steamed brown basmati rice folded with fresh dill and parsley.
instructions: Step 1: Rinse brown basmati rice until water runs clear.\nStep 2: Simmer rice with filtered water and sea salt, covered, for 35–40 minutes until tender; rest 5 minutes off heat.\nStep 3: Fluff with a fork and fold in chopped fresh dill and parsley.

name: Cashew Cream (Base)
description: Thick, dairy-free cream base.
instructions: Step 1: Soak cashews in hot water for 15 minutes.\nStep 2: Drain and blend with 60g filtered water until completely smooth.

name: Cauliflower Florets
description: Fresh raw cauliflower floret clusters
instructions: Clean cruciferous vegetable bases.

name: Chicken Broth
description: A clean, slow-simmered artisanal chicken stock packed with natural collagen and trace micronutrients.

name: Chili Dried
description: Whole dried red chili pods used for sputtering.

name: Chili Powder
description: Ground dried chili pepper blend
instructions: Warm spice seasoning element.

name: Chilli Powder
description: Vibrant ground red chili powder spice blend.

name: Cilantro Lime Dressing (Base)
description: Bright cilantro-lime vinaigrette with garlic and cumin — for bowls, plant dishes, and herb-forward plates.
instructions: Step 1: Finely chop the coriander and mince the garlic.\nStep 2: Whisk olive oil, lime juice, garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper in a jar.\nStep 3: Stir in coriander just before serving.

name: Classic Lemon Garlic Dressing (Base)
description: Everyday lemon-garlic vinaigrette with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
instructions: Step 1: Mince the garlic.\nStep 2: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and pepper until combined.\nStep 3: Toss with salad just before serving.

name: Clove
description: Ground aromatic dried clove buds
instructions: Pungent warm Levantine baking spice.

name: Coconut Chia Pudding (Base)
description: A creamy, mineral-rich base made with healthy fats and fiber, designed for easy meal prep and gut health.
instructions: Step 1: In a glass jar, whisk the chia seeds, honey, and salt into the coconut milk.\nStep 2: Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.\nStep 3: Stir once before serving.

name: Coconut Chicken Curry
instructions: Rich red curry with coconut milk and tender chicken; serve over jasmine rice with fresh Thai basil.

name: Coconut Meat
description: Fresh raw coconut meat, rich in medium-chain triglycerides and fiber.
instructions: N/A

name: Creamy Cajun Sauce (Base)
description: Rich creamy Cajun sauce with coconut cream, fire-roasted tomatoes, peppers, and reserved blackened spice rub.
instructions:
Step 1: Sauté chopped red onion in olive oil for 3 minutes until softened.
Step 2: Add diced red peppers, minced garlic, and reserved Blackened Seasoning (Base); cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Step 3: Add half the chicken broth. Whisk remaining broth with tapioca starch until smooth and add to the pan with drained Fire Roasted Tomatoes (Base), coconut cream, smoked paprika, and lime juice.
Step 4: Simmer, stirring occasionally, until thickened. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, or cayenne.
Step 5: Home batch reference: 4 garlic cloves, 2 red peppers, ½ medium red onion, ½ cup coconut cream, ½ cup well-drained fire-roasted tomatoes, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tbsp lime juice, and chicken broth slurry.

name: Creamy Cumin Hummus (Base)
description: Silky smooth blended chickpeas with rich tahini, garlic, and fresh cumin
instructions:
Step 1: Place cooked chickpeas, minced garlic, sea salt, ground cumin, lemon juice, and tahini into a high-speed food processor.
Step 2: Purée on high for 3 minutes until a thick paste forms.
Step 3: Drop in ice cubes and slowly stream in cold filtered water while running the motor constantly to emulsify.
Step 4: Process for another 2 to 3 minutes until the matrix turns ultra-creamy, pale, and light; chill before serving.

name: Cucumber Pickle (Base)
description: Tangy, quick-pickled cucumber slices.
instructions: Step 1: Slice cucumbers thinly.\nStep 2: Toss with rice vinegar and sea salt.\nStep 3: Let sit for 10 minutes.

name: Curry Powder
description: Aromatic ground yellow curry powder mix
instructions: Warm complex earthy spice profile.

name: Curry Spice Mix
description: Aromatic warming curry powder spice mix
instructions: Clean whole spice curry seasoning.

name: Curry Vinaigrette (Base)
description: Warm curry-spiced vinaigrette for lentil and grain salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, and curry powder.\nStep 2: Toss with salad ingredients.

name: Date Syrup
description: Pure, raw date syrup concentrated from whole dates with no added sugars or preservatives.

name: Dates (Khelas)
description: Soft Gulf khelas dates — natural sweetness for no-bake balls and bars.

name: Dijon Mustard
description: Prepared Dijon mustard with wine vinegar and mustard seed — for marinades and dressings.

name: Egg Whites
description: Pure liquid egg whites with zero yolks
instructions: Lean protein binding agent.

name: Eggplant Dip (Mutabal) (Base)
description: A traditional smoky, creamy roasted eggplant dip emulsified with rich sesame tahini, fresh lemon juice, garlic, and extra virgin olive oil.
instructions: Step 1: Roast the whole eggplant over an open flame or in a 425°F oven until the skin is charred and blackened and the flesh inside is completely soft.\nStep 2: Let cool slightly, then peel off the charred skin and drain any bitter juices from the flesh.\nStep 3: Place the eggplant flesh, minced raw garlic, lemon juice, tahini, sea salt, and extra virgin olive oil into a bowl and mash deeply with a fork or blend until smooth but slightly rustic.

name: Fermented Chimichurri (Base)
description: A deeply complex, robust herb condiment built on an intensive fresh herb blend and developed over a long fermentation cycle.
instructions:
Step 1: Finely mince the fresh dill, parsley, fresh coriander, rosemary, thyme, garlic, and fresh red chili.
Step 2: In a sterile glass fermentation jar, layer the minced herbs, garlic, fresh chilis, and dried chili flakes.
Step 3: Pour in the apple cider vinegar and extra virgin olive oil, making sure all ingredients are completely submerged.
Step 4: Seal with an airtight fermentation lid and let it mature in a cool, dark cupboard for 2-4 weeks to build its characteristic live-culture tang and depth.

name: Fire Roasted Tomatoes (Base)
description: Fire-roasted tomatoes with charred skin peeled off and flesh well drained.
instructions: Step 1: Char whole tomatoes over an open flame or under a hot broiler until the skin is blackened all over.\nStep 2: Transfer to a bowl, cover, and steam 5 minutes; peel away blackened skin.\nStep 3: Core, chop the flesh, and drain well on paper towels before using.

name: Fresh Basil
description: Fresh whole sweet basil leaves
instructions: Fragrant dynamic green herbal component.

name: Fresh Oregano
description: Fresh green oregano leaves
instructions: Aromatic local whole herb element.

name: Fresh Parsley
description: Fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves
instructions: Crisp fresh green herb garnish.

name: Garam Masala
description: Aromatic blend of ground spices used in Indian cuisine.

name: Garden Lemon Herb Dressing (Base)
description: Bright lemon and fresh herb vinaigrette for garden salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil and lemon juice in a small bowl.\nStep 2: Stir in chopped basil and mint.\nStep 3: Use immediately or refrigerate up to 2 days.

name: Garlic & Herb Marinade (Base)
description: Bright garlic-herb lime marinade with dried parsley, chive, tarragon, cilantro, ginger, and chili flakes.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil, lime juice, crushed garlic, dried parsley, chives, tarragon, cilantro, chili flakes, ground ginger, sea salt, and black pepper until combined.\nStep 2: Coat fish or chicken evenly and rest at least 15 minutes before cooking.\nStep 3: Home batch reference (1 serving): 0.04 cup olive oil, 2 cloves garlic, 1 tsp each dried parsley, chive, tarragon, and cilantro, ½ tsp chili flakes, salt and pepper, ½ tsp ground ginger, juice of 1 lime.

name: Garlicky Green Beans (Base)
description: Quick-sautéed green beans with garlic and olive oil — side for mains and bowls.
instructions: Step 1: Trim green beans.\nStep 2: Stir-fry in olive oil with minced garlic until tender-crisp, 3–4 minutes.\nStep 3: Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm.

name: Grapefruit Lime Dressing (Base)
description: Citrus dressing with fresh grapefruit and lime for tropical salads.
instructions: Step 1: Juice any drippings from the grapefruit segments.\nStep 2: Whisk with lime juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper.\nStep 3: Drizzle over salad and serve.

name: Grapefruit Sections
description: Fresh pink grapefruit segments, peeled with no pith
instructions: Tart juicy citrus segments.

name: Green Beans
description: Fresh green snap beans.

name: Green Cardamom
description: Aromatic green cardamom pods — seeds used for curry pastes and warm spice blends.

name: Green Papaya Juice
description: Fresh green papaya juice — natural tenderizer for tandoori marinades.

name: Green Peas
description: Sweet green peas providing clean plant protein and essential dietary fiber.

name: Ground Ginger
description: Fine ground dried ginger root powder
instructions: Zesty ground spice root element.

name: Harissa Paste (Base)
description: House harissa: charred red peppers, garlic, tomatoes, smoked paprika, fresh red chillies, toasted spice mix, and olive oil blended to a brick-red paste.
instructions:
Step 1: Toast the cumin seeds, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, and dried chilli flakes over medium heat in a dry pan until fragrant. Grind in a pestle and mortar until fine and set aside.
Step 2: Char the red bell peppers until skins blacken. Cover and steam, then peel and remove seeds.
Step 3: In a blender or food processor, combine the charred peppers, garlic, chopped tomatoes with their juice, smoked paprika, fresh red chillies, olive oil, ground spice mix, salt, and black pepper. Blend until a brick-red, vibrant paste forms.
Step 4: Season to taste and transfer to jars.

name: Homemade Coconut Milk
description: Fresh, additive-free coconut milk made from whole coconut meat, providing a pure alternative to store-bought.
instructions: Step 1: Blend coconut meat and filtered water on high speed until smooth.\nStep 2: Strain through a nut milk bag into a glass jar.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing (Base)
description: Sweet-sharp honey mustard vinaigrette with date syrup, mustard oil, and lemon.
instructions: Step 1: Mince the garlic.\nStep 2: Whisk olive oil, mustard oil, lemon juice, and date syrup until emulsified.\nStep 3: Stir in garlic, salt, and pepper.

name: Italian Herb Blend
description: Dried blend of oregano, basil, rosemary, and thyme
instructions: Aromatic herb profile.

name: Kashmiri Chili Powder
description: Mild vibrant red kashmiri chili powder for tandoori colour and gentle heat.

name: Lemon Olive Dressing (Base)
description: Simple lemon and olive oil vinaigrette.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil and lemon juice until combined.\nStep 2: Drizzle over salad just before serving.

name: Lemon-Tahini Dressing (Base)
description: Classic lemon-tahini dressing with garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk tahini and lemon juice until smooth.\nStep 2: Stir in minced garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.\nStep 3: Thin with a splash of water if needed until pourable.

name: Lime Herb Dressing (Base)
description: Fresh lime and herb dressing for tropical chicken salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk lime juice and olive oil.\nStep 2: Stir in chopped coriander.\nStep 3: Toss with salad or use as a light marinade.

name: Marinara Sauce (Base)
description: House tomato marinara with soffritto vegetables, roasted red peppers, and fresh herbs.
instructions:
Step 1: Warm olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Step 2: Dice onion, carrot, and celery. Mince garlic. Sauté in the oil until softened (8–10 min).
Step 3: Add chopped tomatoes and simmer 20 minutes until broken down.
Step 4: Prepare Roasted Red Bell Peppers (Base) per base recipe instructions. Stir into the pot with basil, thyme, and parsley. Season with salt and pepper.
Step 5: Simmer 15–20 minutes, then blend until smooth. Adjust seasoning and keep warm.

name: Mediterranean Lemon Dressing (Base)
description: Classic Mediterranean lemon vinaigrette.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil and lemon juice.\nStep 2: Toss with salad just before serving.

name: Mint Coconut Chutney Dressing (Base)
description: Spicy Indian-style coconut-lime chutney dressing with mint and coriander.
instructions: Step 1: Finely chop mint, coriander, and chilli.\nStep 2: Whisk coconut milk, lime juice, olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper.\nStep 3: Stir in herbs and chilli. Tag: Spicy.

name: Miso Tahini Dressing (Base)
description: Savory miso-tahini dressing with sesame oil and rice vinegar.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk tahini, miso, vinegar, and sesame oil until smooth.\nStep 2: Stir in garlic and thin with water to pourable.

name: Moroccan Spice Blend (Cumin Cinnamon Coriander)
description: Custom North African spice blend
instructions: Aromatic blend of ground cumin, cinnamon, and coriander seed.

name: Mustard Dressing (Base)
description: A sharp, zesty non-toxic dressing utilizing clean fats and a punch of warm mustard oil.
instructions: Step 1: Mince the raw garlic finely.\nStep 2: Whisk the extra virgin olive oil, mustard oil, and fresh lemon juice together in a small bowl.\nStep 3: Stir in the minced garlic, sea salt, and black pepper until completely emulsified.

name: Nutmeg
description: Whole or freshly ground organic nutmeg kernels
instructions: Intensely aromatic and warm seed seasoning profile.

name: Onion Powder
description: Dried ground onion — for rubs, spice blends, and savoury seasoning.

name: Orange Sections

instructions: Juicy citrus segments.

name: Oregano
description: Premium, aromatic dried oregano leaves high in iron and antioxidants for clean seasoning.

name: Peanut Butter Dressing (Base)
description: Thai-inspired peanut dressing with tamari, sesame, ginger, garlic, and date syrup.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk peanut butter with rice vinegar, date syrup, tamari, and sesame oil until smooth.\nStep 2: Stir in ginger, garlic, and chilli sauce.\nStep 3: Thin with water to a pourable consistency.

name: Peanuts (Crushed)
description: Raw crushed peanut pieces
instructions: Crunchy topping allocation.

name: Pickled Red Onion (Base)
description: Tangy, vibrant pickled red onions.
instructions: Step 1: Slice onions into thin rings.\nStep 2: Submerge in apple cider vinegar and salt.\nStep 3: Let sit for 20 minutes before serving.

name: Pineapple
description: Raw fresh pineapple chunks, sweet and highly enzymatic.

name: Pomegranate Juice
description: Pure, unsweetened fresh pomegranate juice, tart and deeply antioxidant-rich.

name: Pomegranate Seeds
description: Fresh pomegranate arils, vibrant red and juicy with a crisp seed center.

name: Pomegranate Sumac Sauce (Base)
description: A tart pomegranate-sumac marinade with garlic and thyme for sumac chicken skewers.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk together the olive oil, pomegranate juice, lemon juice, tomato paste, sumac, black pepper, and sea salt until well combined.\nStep 2: Stir in the coarsely chopped garlic cloves and fresh thyme sprigs.\nStep 3: Pour into a large ziplock bag with the chicken, massage everything together, and marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight.

name: Protein Powder (Isolate)
description: Clean unflavored whey or plant isolate protein powder
instructions: Pure protein isolate reinforcement.

name: Pumpkin
description: Fresh pumpkin — cubed and roasted for Thai curries and autumn plates.

name: Quinoa (Base)
description: Perfectly cooked, fluffy white quinoa seasoned lightly with clean sea salt to serve as a versatile meal base.
instructions:
Step 1: Rinse quinoa thoroughly under cold water.
Step 2: Bring filtered water and sea salt to a boil in a saucepan.
Step 3: Add quinoa, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Step 4: Remove from heat and let sit covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

name: Quinoa Bread (Base)
description: A dense, clean, and fiber-rich gluten-free alternative bread made entirely from wholesome whole quinoa flour base.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk the dry quinoa flour, sea salt, and baking powder together thoroughly in a mixing bowl.\nStep 2: Stream in the filtered water and extra virgin olive oil, mixing continuously until a thick, structured batter forms.\nStep 3: Transfer the batter mixture into a prepared mini loaf pan and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25 to 30 minutes until fully set and the top crust turns golden brown.

name: Quinoa Flatbread (Base)
description: A simple, two-ingredient gluten-free flatbread made from wholesome quinoa flour, pan-seared until light, pliable, and perfectly tender for dipping.
instructions:
Step 1: In a small mixing bowl, whisk together the quinoa flour and sea salt.
Step 2: Pour in the filtered water and half of the olive oil, stirring continuously to form a smooth, workable dough.
Step 3: Divide dough into balls and roll flat between two sheets of parchment paper.
Step 4: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat with the remaining olive oil, and cook the flatbread for 2 to 3 minutes per side until lightly golden brown and flexible.

name: Raisins
description: Unsweetened naturally dried purple grapes
instructions: Whole food sweetness elements.

name: Ratatouille (Base)
description: A rich, deeply flavored Mediterranean vegetable stew slow-simmered with sweet peppers, onions, eggplant, zucchini, Harissa Paste (Base), savory anchovies, and fresh basil.
instructions:
Step 1: Halve zucchini lengthways and slice eggplant 1cm thick, then roast at 400°F until tender.
Step 2: Chop roughly.
Step 3: Chop peppers and onions, then sauté in a pan with Harissa Paste (Base), anchovies, and a teaspoon of their oil.
Step 4: Add crushed garlic.
Step 5: Stir in roasted zucchini, eggplant, tomato paste, and vinegar.
Step 6: Simmer with the lid on until flavors meld, finishing with fresh torn basil leaves.

name: Red Chili
description: Fresh hot red chili peppers, providing vibrant heat and high vitamin C capsicum notes.

name: Red Pepper Dressing (Base)
description: A vibrant, emulsified sweet red pepper dressing balanced with zesty apple cider vinegar and clean extra virgin olive oil.
instructions:
Step 1: Roughly chop the bell pepper, white onion, and garlic.
Step 2: Place them into a high-speed blender along with the apple cider vinegar, sea salt, and black pepper.
Step 3: Blend on high until completely smooth.
Step 4: Lower the blender speed and slowly stream in the extra virgin olive oil until the dressing is fully emulsified.

name: Red Thai Curry Paste (Base)
description: House-made red Thai curry paste with toasted spices, aromatics, fish sauce, and coconut milk.
instructions:
Step 1: Toast coriander seeds, green cardamom, dry red chilies, black peppercorns, and cumin in a dry pan until fragrant; grind to a fine powder.
Step 2: Roughly chop garlic, shallots, galangal, lemongrass (lower half of stalks), coriander stems, and Thai chilies.
Step 3: Blend the chopped aromatics with lime zest, lime juice, lime leaves, ground spices, sesame oil, and filtered water until a smooth paste forms.
Step 4: Stir in fish sauce and coconut milk until fully combined.
Step 5: Store refrigerated and use as the base for red Thai curries.

name: Rice Flour
description: Finely milled gluten-free white rice flour
instructions: Used as a non-toxic binding matrix for fritters and bakes.

name: Roasted Cherry Tomato (Base)
description: Slow-roasted, herb-infused sweet cherry tomatoes concentrated in flavor with clean fats.
instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Step 2: Toss cherry tomatoes with extra virgin olive oil, oregano, thyme, and sea salt.
Step 3: Spread evenly on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Step 4: Roast for 20-25 minutes until the skins are wrinkled and blistered.
Step 5: Cool and store with the released cooking juices.

name: Roasted Mixed Vegetables (Base)
description: Roasted koosa, eggplant, and butternut squash with sea salt, rosemary, and thyme.
instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a tray with parchment paper.
Step 2: Dice koosa, eggplant, and butternut squash into 2–3 cm cubes.
Step 3: Toss with olive oil, sea salt, rosemary, and thyme until evenly coated.
Step 4: Spread in a single layer and roast 25–30 minutes until tender and golden at the edges.
Step 5: Serve warm as a side or fold into mains.

name: Roasted Red Bell Peppers (Base)
description: Two roasted red bell peppers with oregano — sub-base for marinara and tomato sauces.
instructions:
Step 1: Halve the red bell peppers and remove seeds and stems.
Step 2: Toss with olive oil, oregano, sea salt, and black pepper.
Step 3: Roast at 200°C until charred and tender (25–30 min).
Step 4: Cool, peel if desired, and reserve the flesh and any juices.

name: Rocca
description: Fresh, peppery green rocca leaves (arugula) rich in Vitamin K and calcium for clean salad bases.

name: Rosemary Garlic Chicken (Base)
description: Premium grilled chicken breast marinated with garlic, rosemary, Dijon mustard, and lemon.
instructions:
Step 1: Mince the garlic and finely chop the rosemary leaves.
Step 2: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper into a marinade.
Step 3: Rub the marinade evenly over the chicken breast and rest at least 15 minutes.
Step 4: Sear on a hot grill or heavy skillet for 5–6 minutes per side until charred and the internal temperature hits 74°C.
Step 5: Rest for 5 minutes before slicing.

name: Saffron Rice (Base)
description: Fragrant, bright yellow steamed basmati rice infused with a premium pinch of genuine saffron and balanced with a clean hint of fresh lemon citrus.
instructions: Step 1: Combine white basmati rice, filtered water, a good pinch of saffron, half a lemon shell, and a pinch of sea salt in a small saucepan.\nStep 2: Cover tightly and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and steam until fluffy, stirring occasionally.\nStep 3: Remove lemon shell before serving.

name: Saffron Threads
description: Premium local saffron stigma threads
instructions: Genuine local saffron for golden color and luxury aroma.

name: Sauteed Mint Basil Koosa (Base)
description: Pan-seared koosa rounds tossed with fresh hand-torn mint and basil leaves
instructions: Step 1: Sauté uniform koosa squash coins in extra virgin olive oil over medium-high heat until skin edges char and turn deep golden.\nStep 2: Remove from heat source and fold fresh soft mint and sweet basil leaves directly over the hot squash matrix.

name: Shawarma Spice Blend
description: Levant shawarma spice mix
instructions: A clean aromatic blend of cumin, allspice, garlic, and cinnamon.

name: Shrimp (Raw)
description: Raw wild-caught clean shrimp
instructions: Premium lean marine protein element.

name: Slaw (Base)
description: Simple shredded cabbage and carrot slaw.
instructions: Step 1: Finely shred the purple cabbage and grate the carrots.\nStep 2: Toss together with vinegar and sea salt until combined.

name: Smashed White Beans (Base)
description: Silky white beans slowly simmered and infused with garlic and olive oil emulsion.
instructions:
Step 1: Soak dry cannellini beans in 4 liters of filtered water for 12 hours; drain and rinse thoroughly.
Step 2: Place soaked beans in a large pot with 3 liters of fresh water; bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a very low simmer for 60 minutes.
Step 3: Once bean bodies are perfectly tender, remove from heat and drain out excess liquid.
Step 4: Stir in sautéed garlic, sea salt, black pepper, and fresh lemon juice while the beans are hot.
Step 5: Stream extra virgin olive oil slowly into the pot while folding gently; smash 20% of the bean base with a wooden mallet to finish.

name: Smoked Paprika
description: Deeply aromatic Spanish smoked paprika.

name: Spiced Aleppo Ground Beef (Base)
description: Warm pan-seared ground beef heavily aromatic with Aleppo pepper and warm Levant spices
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a wide skillet. Add diced red onions and sauté for 5 minutes. 2. Add ground beef, breaking it up fine. 3. Shake all spices evenly over the meat. 4. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes until fully browned.

name: Spiced Lemon Dressing (Base)
description: Cumin and lemon dressing for roasted vegetable salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.\nStep 2: Use half to roast vegetables and half to finish the salad.

name: Spicy Green Chilli Sauce (Base)
description: A vibrant, fiery green condiment blended with fresh herbs, garlic, citrus, and toasted spices.
instructions: Step 1: Toast black peppercorns, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and cardamom seeds in a dry pan until fragrant, then grind.\nStep 2: In a high-speed blender, combine the ground spices, sea salt, cilantro, parsley, chopped green chiles, dried chiles, crushed garlic, and lemon juice.\nStep 3: Stream in extra virgin olive oil slowly while blending until a uniform sauce forms.

name: Steamed Basmati Rice (Base)
description: Perfectly fluffy, clean-steamed white basmati rice lightly seasoned with mineral-rich sea salt.
instructions:
Step 1: Thoroughly rinse white basmati rice under cold water until the water runs completely clear to remove excess surface starch.
Step 2: In a heavy-bottomed pot, bring filtered water and sea salt to a rolling boil.
Step 3: Stir in the rinsed basmati rice, reduce the heat to low, cover tightly with a lid, and let it simmer undisturbed for 12-15 minutes.
Step 4: Remove the pot from the heat source and let it sit covered for an additional 5 minutes to complete steam absorption.
Step 5: Uncover, fluff gently with a fork, and scale per portion allocation.

name: Sumac Za'atar Dressing (Base)
description: Levantine dressing with sumac, za'atar, lemon, and olive oil.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, sumac, za'atar, salt, and pepper.\nStep 2: Rest 10 minutes before tossing with salad.

name: Tamari
description: Premium gluten-free Japanese tamari soy sauce.

name: Tamari Sauce
description: Gluten-free tamari soy sauce
instructions: Traditional naturally fermented gluten-free soy sauce.

name: Tandoori Chicken (Base)
description: Tandoori-marinated chicken breast using house tandoori spice mix, lemon, and coconut.
instructions:
Step 1: Coat chicken evenly with Tandoori Spice Mix (Base), lemon juice, coconut milk, and olive oil.
Step 2: Marinate at least 4 hours or overnight.
Step 3: Grill or pan-sear until golden then bake in the oven for 20 minutes exactly.
Step 4: Rest and slice.

name: Tandoori Coconut Mint Dressing (Base)
description: Cool coconut mint dressing with tandoori spice.
instructions: Step 1: Finely chop mint and coconut.\nStep 2: Whisk with lime juice, olive oil, and tandoori spice.\nStep 3: Marinate chicken in half, then dress the salad with the rest.

name: Tandoori Lime Dressing (Base)
description: Smoky tandoori lime dressing for chicken salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk lime juice, olive oil, and tandoori spice.\nStep 2: Marinate chicken in half the dressing, then use the rest to finish the salad.

name: Tandoori Salmon (Base)
description: Tandoori-marinated salmon fillet with house spice mix and citrus.
instructions: Step 1: Rub salmon with Tandoori Spice Mix (Base), lemon juice, coconut milk, and olive oil.\nStep 2: Marinate 30–60 minutes.\nStep 3: Grill or bake at 200°C until just cooked through. Rest briefly before serving.

name: Tandoori Spice Mix
description: Bold aromatic Northern Indian spice blend
instructions: Clean whole spice tandoori rub matrix.

name: Tandoori Spice Mix (Base)
description: Authentic tandoori masala paste with kashmiri chili, green papaya juice, black tamarind, and warm whole spices.
instructions:
Step 1: Lightly toast brown cardamom, anise seeds, and cumin powder in a dry pan until fragrant.
Step 2: Grind with paprika, kashmiri chili, garlic powder, ginger powder, cinnamon, mace, cane sugar, salt, and pepper.
Step 3: Work in green papaya juice and black tamarind until a thick rub forms.
Step 4: Use for all tandoori chicken and fish marinades.

name: Thai Peanut Dressing (Base)
description: Creamy peanut lime dressing for slaws and rainbow salads.
instructions: Step 1: Whisk peanut butter with lime juice until smooth.\nStep 2: Thin with water to a pourable consistency.\nStep 3: Toss with prepared vegetables.

name: Thyme
description: Dried thyme leaves — earthy herb for rubs, roasts, and spice blends.

name: Thyme (Fresh)
description: Fresh sprigs of thyme, deeply aromatic and wood-sy.

name: Turmeric Lemon Dressing (Base)
description: Golden turmeric lemon dressing for kale and chicken salads.
instructions: Step 1: Mince garlic and whisk with olive oil, lemon juice, turmeric, salt, and pepper.\nStep 2: Use half to marinate chicken and half to dress the salad.

name: Turmeric Powder
description: Pure ground turmeric root powder, vibrant yellow and deeply anti-inflammatory.

name: Turmeric Rice (Base)
description: Bright, golden-hued long grain basmati rice steamed with aromatic ground turmeric root and balanced with sea salt.
instructions: Step 1: Rinse white basmati rice thoroughly until the water runs completely clear.\nStep 2: In a small saucepan, combine the washed rice, filtered water, ground turmeric powder, and sea salt, stirring once to distribute the golden spice evenly.\nStep 3: Bring the pot to a rolling boil over high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to low, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and let steam undisturbed for 15 minutes until fluffy.

name: Vegan Worcestershire Sauce
description: Gluten-free, plant-based savory Worcestershire sauce.

name: Vegetable Broth (Base)
description: A rich, clear vegetable reduction broth made entirely from whole foods and aromatic herbs.
instructions:
Step 1: Roast vegetables lightly.
Step 2: Combine all ingredients in a stainless steel stockpot with filtered water.
Step 3: Simmer on low heat for 4 hours.
Step 4: Strain through fine mesh and cool immediately.

name: Zesty Lime Chili Salad Dressing (Base)
description: A vibrant, punchy vinaigrette matching rich extra virgin olive oil with fresh tart lime juice, crushed red pepper flakes, and a hint of cider vinegar.
instructions: Step 1: Combine the extra virgin olive oil, freshly squeezed lime juice, apple cider vinegar, crushed red pepper flakes, sea salt, and black pepper in a small bowl or sealable jar.\nStep 2: Whisk or shake vigorously until the dynamic acid and oil layers emulsify into a smooth dressing paste.

name: coriander powder
description: Fine ground coriander seed powder, warm and mildly citrusy.

name: cumin powder
description: Fine ground cumin seed powder, deeply aromatic and earthy.

name: mustard seeds
description: Whole mustard seeds used for tempering and sputtering in hot oil.
